questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two radioactive nuclides $X$ and $Y$ have half lives of 100 min and 20 min respectively. In a sample, the ratio of nuclides of $X$ : $Y$ was found to be $1: 8$. Calculate the time after which the ratio of number of nuclides of $X$ become twice that of Y .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider an endothermic reaction
$\mathrm{A} \underset{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}{\stackrel{\mathrm{K}_1}{\rightleftharpoons}} \mathrm{~B} \quad \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{f}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$ are the forward and the backward rate constant at temperature $\mathrm{T}_1$. The temperature is decreased from $T_1$ to $T_2$ and the rate constants are $K_f^{\prime}$ and $K_b^{\prime}$ for the forward and the backward reaction respectively at this temperature. Select the incorrect relation.
questions.solution: As temperature decreases rate constant decreases. So, $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}^{\prime} \lt \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}{ }^{\prime} \lt \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$. The rate constant with greater activation energy is more sensitive towards temperature So, \% decrease in $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}$ will be greater than \% decrease in $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The ratio $t_{1 / 2}: t_{3 / 4}$ of a reaction having order equal to ' $n$ ' $(n \neq 1)$ is
( $\mathrm{t}_{3 / 4}$ is the time required for concentration of reactant to drop to $\frac{3}{4}$ times its initial value)
options: Dependent on initial concentration $\left[A_0\right]$ and equal to $\frac{2^{n-1}\left[A_0\right]^{n-1}}{\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)^{n-1}}$
Dependent on initial concentration $\left[A_0\right]$ and equal to $\frac{2^{n-1}\left[A_0\right]^{n-1}-1}{\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^{n-1}\left[A_0\right]^{n-1}-1}$
Independent of initial concentration and equal to $\frac{2^{n-1}-1}{\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^{n-1}-1}$
Independent of initial concentration and equal to $\frac{n \cdot 2^{n-1}-1}{n \cdot\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^{n-1}-1}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A reactant dissociates into different products in concurrent elementary reactions as given below
$\begin{aligned}
& P \xrightarrow{\mathrm{~K}_1} 2 Q, \mathrm{~K}_1=15 \times 10^{-2} \\
& 3 \mathrm{P} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{~K}_2} 4 \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{~K}_2=5 \times 10^{-2} \\
& \mathrm{nP} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{~K}_3} 3 \mathrm{~S}, \mathrm{~K}_3=7.5 \times 10^{-2}
\end{aligned}$

The value of $\mathrm{K}_1, \mathrm{~K}_2$ and $\mathrm{K}_3$ given are magnitude only. If the initial rate of disappearance of ' P ' when the pressure of $P$ is 1 atm is $0.45 \frac{\mathrm{~atm}}{\mathrm{sec}}$ the value of $n$ is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the given statements is correct?
options: Every species that appears in the rate law must be a reactant of the reaction
Every species that appears in the rate law must be a reactant or product of the reaction
Products can never appear in the rate law of the reaction
All homogeneous reaction rates have the same dimension
questions.solution: All homogenous reaction rates have the dimension of concentration divided by time. Catalyst may be involved in rate law.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
